commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=2d886f65a0a97988265d7935aa17378152458f8a)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a new OpenCode Zen provider catalog and registers dynamic model resolution, catalog augmentation, and base URL normalization hooks for the bundled opencode plugin.

PR surface: Source +183. Total +183 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence path: current docs configure opencode/... Zen refs, current main lacks the dynamic/catalog hooks, and the PR's new DeepSeek row conflicts with the existing live-test model metadata. I did not run a live OpenCode turn in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor/live proof and maintainer review of Zen catalog metadata before merge; automation cannot supply the external setup proof for the contributor.

target.title: feat(opencode): add resolveDynamicModel and augmentModelCatalog hooks to Zen plugin
target.body:
## Problem

The `opencode` (Zen) provider plugin, introduced in the OpenCode provider-key split, is missing `resolveDynamicModel` and `augmentModelCatalog` runtime hooks that **every other bundled provider implements**.

Without these hooks, the agent runtime **cannot resolve `opencode/<model-id>` references** at runtime. When a model like `opencode/deepseek-v4-flash-free` is configured in `agents.defaults.models`, the resolution chain:

1. ❌ Checks `modelRegistry.find("opencode", "deepseek-v4-flash-free")` — not found
2. ❌ Calls `plugin.resolveDynamicModel("opencode", "deepseek-v4-flash-free")` — **undefined** (hook not implemented)
3. ❌ Falls through to `resolveConfiguredFallbackModel` — requires explicit `models.providers["opencode"]` config entry
4. ❌ **"Unknown model" error**

Users hit this as `⚠️ Unknown model: opencode/deepseek-v4-flash-free` when trying to use the Zen proxy through fallback chains, requiring fragile config workarounds (manual `models.providers` entries or models.json manipulation) that are not needed for any other provider.

**Severity:** Medium — annoyance that requires manual config work for every Zen model the user wants to reference in the fallback chain.

## Solution

Add the three missing runtime hooks to the `opencode` Zen provider plugin, mirroring the pattern established by `opencode-go` at `extensions/opencode-go/provider-catalog.ts`:

### New file: `extensions/opencode/provider-catalog.ts`

- Static model catalog with known Zen-proxied models (deepseek-v4-flash-free, Claude Opus 4.6–4.7, Sonnet 4.6, Gemini 2.5 Pro)
- `listOpencodeZenModelCatalogEntries()` — returns discoverable catalog entries
- `resolveOpencodeZenModel(modelId)` — resolves a model by ID at runtime
- `normalizeOpencodeZenBaseUrl()` — normalizes ``https://opencode.ai/zen/v1`` base URL (also accepts bare `https://opencode.ai` and `https://opencode.ai/zen`)
- `normalizeOpencodeZenResolvedModel()` — normalizes resolved model base URLs

### Modified: `extensions/opencode/index.ts`

- `resolveDynamicModel` → delegates to `resolveOpencodeZenModel()`
- `augmentModelCatalog` → delegates to `listOpencodeZenModelCatalogEntries()`
- `normalizeConfig` → normalizes provider config base URLs
- `normalizeResolvedModel` → normalizes resolved model base URLs
- `normalizeTransport` → normalizes transport-level base URLs

### Resolution chain after fix

1. ✅ `modelRegistry.find("opencode", "deepseek-v4-flash-free")` — found (catalog provides it)
2. ✅ `plugin.resolveDynamicModel("opencode", "deepseek-v4-flash-free")` — resolved at runtime
3. (Escape hatch) No config workaround needed

## Verification

- `pnpm build` — ✅ clean build
- `pnpm test -- extensions/opencode` — **13/13 tests passed** across 5 test files (index.test.ts, onboard.test.ts, media-understanding-provider.test.ts, provider-policy-api.test.ts, plugin-registration.contract.test.ts)
- Rebased on `upstream/main` (no merge conflicts)
- Cherry-picked on a clean branch from `upstream/main` — no unrelated commits

## Why this matters

The `opencode` Zen provider is a multi-model proxy (Claude, GPT, Gemini, DeepSeek). Without `resolveDynamicModel`, every model added to the proxy requires a manual `models.providers` config entry — creating a poor developer experience and an ongoing maintenance burden as new models become available. With the hooks in place, models are automatically discoverable and resolvable, exactly like every other bundled provider.
target.author: olisikh
